services.name: bakufu-shared
services.description: Common auth, config, and Swagger-driven endpoint conventions.

services.name: bakufu-login
services.description: The authorization process involves obtaining an access token and a refresh token. For details on the authorization process and security settings, see [Authorization and Security](https://helpcenter.veeam.com/references/vbr/13/rest/1.3-rev1/tag/SectionOverview#section/Authorization-and-Security).

services.name: bakufu-service
services.description: The Service section defines paths and operations for retrieving information about the backup server where the REST API service is running.

services.name: bakufu-services
services.description: The Services section defines a path and operation for retrieving information about associated backend services. You may need to connect to these services for integration with Veeam Backup & Replication.

services.name: bakufu-credentials
services.description: The Credentials section defines paths and operations for managing credentials records that are added to the backup server.

services.name: bakufu-encryption
services.description: The Encryption section defines paths and operations for managing KMS servers and passwords that are used for data encryption.

services.name: bakufu-license
services.description: The License section defines paths and operations for installing and managing licenses on the backup server.

services.name: bakufu-connection
services.description: The Connection section defines a path and operation for retrieving a TLS certificate or SSH fingerprint used to establish a secure connection between the backup server and the specified server.

services.name: bakufu-cloud-browser
services.description: The Cloud Browser section defines paths and operations for retrieving information about cloud compute and storage resources. The Cloud Browser section helps you map a cloud folder with an object storage repository.

services.name: bakufu-inventory-browser
services.description: The Inventory Browser section defines paths and operations that allow you to: Retrieve virtualization servers and their virtual infrastructure objects (data centers, clusters, hosts, resource pools, vApps, VMs and so on). In this version, you can browse VMware vSphere, VMware Cloud Director and Microsoft Hyper-V objects. Manage computers, cloud machines, clusters and Active Directory objects with protection groups. Manage unstructured data sources. Manage Microsoft Entra ID tenants.

services.name: bakufu-traffic-rules
services.description: The Traffic Rules section defines paths and operations for retrieving and editing information about network traffic rules that are configured on the backup server.

services.name: bakufu-general-options
services.description: The General Options section defines paths and operations for retrieving and editing general settings of Veeam Backup & Replication.

services.name: bakufu-users-and-roles
services.description: The Users and Roles section defines paths and operations for managing Veeam Backup & Replication users, groups and assigned roles.

services.name: bakufu-global-exclusions
services.description: The Global Exclusions section defines paths and operations for managing which VMs will be excluded from processing, even if they are included in jobs. In this version, you can use these operations for VMware vSphere, VMware Cloud Director and Microsoft Hyper-V VMs.

services.name: bakufu-security
services.description: The Security section defines paths and operations for managing Security & Compliance Analyzer and getting four-eyes authorization events.

services.name: bakufu-malware-detection
services.description: The Malware Detection section defines paths and operations for managing malware events and scanning backups with antivirus software or YARA rules.

services.name: bakufu-configuration-backup
services.description: The Configuration Backup section defines paths and operations for managing backup of the configuration database that Veeam Backup & Replication uses.

services.name: bakufu-deployment
services.description: The Deployment section defines paths and operations for generating and downloading a deployment kit that is used for adding Microsoft Windows machines as managed servers.

services.name: bakufu-managed-servers
services.description: The Managed Servers section defines paths and operations for managing servers. NOTE In this version, the REST API supports the following server types: *VMware vSphere*, *Hyper-V*, *Linux* and *Windows*.

services.name: bakufu-repositories
services.description: The Repositories section defines paths and operations for managing backup repositories. NOTE In this version, the REST API supports the following storage types: direct attached storage (Microsoft Windows and Linux servers), network attached storage (SMB and NFS shares), object storage repositories and Veeam Data Cloud Vault repositories. For details on how to add an object storage repository, see [Object Storage Repositories](https://helpcenter.veeam.com/references/vbr/13/rest/1.3-rev1/tag/SectionHowTo#section/Object-Storage-Repositories).

services.name: bakufu-mount-servers
services.description: The Mount Servers section defines paths and operations for managing mount servers in your backup infrastructure. Mount servers are used in advanced restore operations (file restore, application item restore and Instant Recovery).

services.name: bakufu-proxies
services.description: The Proxies section defines paths and operations for managing backup proxies. NOTE In this version, the REST API supports VMware vSphere backup proxies, Microsoft Hyper-V off-host backup proxies and general-purpose backup proxies used with Veeam Agents and unstructured data.

services.name: bakufu-wan-accelerators
services.description: The WAN Accelerators section defines paths and operations for managing WAN accelerators.

services.name: bakufu-jobs
services.description: The Jobs section defines paths and operations for managing jobs that are coordinated by the backup server. NOTE In this version, the REST API supports the following job types: `VSphereBackup` — VMware vSphere backup jobs. `vSphereReplica` — VMware vSphere replication jobs. `HyperVBackup` — Microsoft Hyper-V backup jobs. `CloudDirectorBackup` — VMware Cloud Director backup jobs. `WindowsAgentBackup` — Veeam Agent backup jobs for Microsoft Windows computers. `LinuxAgentBackup` — Veeam Agent backup jobs for Linux computers. `WindowsAgentBackupWorkstationPolicy` — Veeam Agent backup policies for Microsoft Windows computers of the workstation type. `LinuxAgentBackupWorkstationPolicy` — Veeam Agent backup policies for Linux computers of the workstation type. `WindowsAgentBackupServerPolicy` — Veeam Agent backup policies for Linux computers of the server type. `LinuxAgentBackupServerPolicy` — Veeam Agent backup policies for Linux computers of the server type. `EntraIDTenantBackup` — Microsoft Entra ID tenant backup jobs. `EntraIDAuditLogBackup` — Microsoft Entra ID audit log backup jobs. `FileBackup` — File share backup jobs. `ObjectStorageBackup` — Object storage backup jobs. `SureBackupContentScan` — SureBackup Lite jobs that perform backup verification and content scan only (full recoverability testing in virtual labs is not available). In the current version, SureBackup is supported only for backups created with Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure, Veeam Backup for AWS, or Veeam Backup for Google Cloud. `BackupCopy` — VMware vSphere and Microsoft Hyper-V backup copy jobs. `FileBackupCopy` — Backup copy jobs for file shares. VMware vSphere jobs can process inventory objects of the following types: *VirtualMachine*, *vCenterServer*, *Datacenter*, *Cluster*, *Host*, *ResourcePool*, *Folder*, *Template*, *Tag*, *Datastore* and *DatastoreCluster*. Inventory objects with multiple tags (*Multitag* type) can only be added to jobs in the Veeam Backup & Replication UI or PowerShell — the REST API only allows you to get and manage jobs that process these objects.

services.name: bakufu-backups
services.description: The Backups section defines paths and operations for managing backups that are created on or imported to the backup server.

services.name: bakufu-backup-objects
services.description: The Backup Objects section defines paths and operations for managing backup objects — VMs and vApps that are included in backups created by the backup server. Types of backup objects differ depending on the backup job platform: Backups of VMware vSphere jobs contain VMs only. Backups of VMware Cloud Director jobs contain VMs and vApps.

services.name: bakufu-restore-points
services.description: The Restore Points section defines paths and operations for retrieving restore points created on the backup server and backed up disks from the restore points.

services.name: bakufu-restore
services.description: The Restore section defines paths and operations for performing restore. NOTE In this version, the REST API supports the following recovery operations: Instant Recovery of a VMware vSphere VM to VMware vSphere Instant Recovery of a Microsoft Hyper-V VM to Microsoft Hyper-V Instant Recovery of Microsoft Windows and Linux machines to Microsoft Azure Instant File Share Recovery to the original or another location Entire VM restore of a VMware vSphere VM to VMware vSphere Entire VM restore of a Microsoft Hyper-V VM to Microsoft Hyper-V Entire VM restore of a VMware Cloud Director VM to VMware Cloud Director Restore of an entire file share, bucket or container to the original or another location Restore of disks that will be registered as First Class Disks (FCD) — a type of virtual disks that can be managed independent of any VM File restore from a backup or replica of a Microsoft Windows or Linux machine File restore from an unstructured data backup Microsoft Entra ID item restore Microsoft Entra ID audit log restore

services.name: bakufu-data-integration-api
services.description: The Data Integration API section defines paths and operations for publishing disks from backups and snapshot replicas.

services.name: bakufu-backup-browsers
services.description: The Backup Browser section defines paths and operations that allow you to: Browse the file system, compare files and folders, and perform file restore. Browse Microsoft Entra ID tenant backups, compare Microsoft Entra ID items, and restore entire items or their properties. Browse the Microsoft Entra ID log files, compare files and folders, and restore Microsoft Entra ID logs.

services.name: bakufu-tasks
services.description: The Tasks section defines paths and operations for managing tasks that are used to perform runtime operations (file restore and rescan of inventory objects).

services.name: bakufu-replicas
services.description: The Replicas section defines paths and operations for managing snapshot replicas.

services.name: bakufu-replica-restore-points
services.description: The Replica Restore Points section defines paths and operations for managing restore points of snapshot replicas.

services.name: bakufu-failover
services.description: The Failover section defines paths and operations for managing failover.

services.name: bakufu-failback
services.description: The Failback section defines paths and operations for managing failback.

services.name: bakufu-sessions
services.description: The Sessions section defines paths and operations for managing sessions (except [automation sessions](Automation#operation/GetAllAutomationSessions)) and task sessions performed on the backup server.

services.name: bakufu-agents
services.description: The Agents section defines paths and operations for working with protection groups, obtaining protected computers, managing Veeam Agent and other Veeam components on the computers and handling recovery tokens used for bare metal recovery.

services.name: bakufu-active-directory-domains
services.description: The Active Directory Domains section defines paths and operations for managing Active Directory domains.

services.name: bakufu-automation
services.description: The Automation section defines paths and operations for granular import and export of objects available in the REST API. It can be useful, for example, if you set up the backup infrastructure using JSON specification or migrate the infrastructure to another backup server. For details, see [Mass Deployment](https://helpcenter.veeam.com/references/vbr/13/rest/1.3-rev1/tag/SectionHowTo#section/Mass-Deployment).

services.name: bakufu-log-export
services.description: The Log Export section defines paths and operations for exporting the backup server logs.

helpers.name: bakufu-jobs-start-by-name
helpers.description: Start a job by name and return session details.

helpers.name: bakufu-jobs-last-result
helpers.description: Get latest job session result and status.

helpers.name: bakufu-session-follow
helpers.description: Poll a session until terminal state.

helpers.name: bakufu-session-logs
helpers.description: Fetch session logs and surface failures.

helpers.name: bakufu-repo-capacity
helpers.description: Summarize repository capacity and free space.

helpers.name: bakufu-repo-add-wasabi
helpers.description: Create a Wasabi object storage repository.

helpers.name: bakufu-cloud-credentials-add
helpers.description: Create cloud credentials for S3-compatible storage.

helpers.name: bakufu-object-storage-browse
helpers.description: Browse object storage regions, buckets, and folders.

helpers.name: bakufu-job-by-name
helpers.description: Resolve a job id from a job name.

helpers.name: bakufu-session-last-failed
helpers.description: Find the most recent failed session for a job.

helpers.name: bakufu-security-analyzer-last-run
helpers.description: Get the latest Security Analyzer run state and findings.

personas.name: persona-backup-admin
personas.description: Design and govern backup infrastructure and policy.

personas.name: persona-backup-operator
personas.description: Run daily backup operations and resolve failed jobs.

personas.name: persona-security-admin
personas.description: Operate security analyzer, malware detection, and hardening controls.

personas.name: persona-dr-operator
personas.description: Validate restore readiness and execute DR runbooks.

personas.name: persona-auditor
personas.description: Collect compliance evidence and produce operational audit summaries.

recipes.name: recipe-investigate-failed-job
recipes.description: Find the latest failed job session and collect logs.

recipes.name: recipe-rerun-failed-job
recipes.description: Start a failed job again and monitor session completion.

recipes.name: recipe-daily-job-health
recipes.description: Summarize success, warning, and failed jobs in the last 24 hours.

recipes.name: recipe-weekly-job-health
recipes.description: Generate a weekly trend summary of job results.

recipes.name: recipe-capacity-report
recipes.description: Produce repository capacity, free-space, and usage report.

recipes.name: recipe-validate-immutability
recipes.description: Verify object storage immutability settings and retention posture.

recipes.name: recipe-create-wasabi-repo
recipes.description: Create Wasabi credentials and repository mapping.

recipes.name: recipe-repository-rescan
recipes.description: Rescan repositories and capture result states.

recipes.name: recipe-repository-online-check
recipes.description: List repositories and flag offline or out-of-date states.

recipes.name: recipe-security-analyzer-run
recipes.description: Run Security Analyzer and summarize findings.

recipes.name: recipe-security-analyzer-schedule-review
recipes.description: Inspect Security Analyzer schedule configuration.

recipes.name: recipe-best-practices-review
recipes.description: Collect and summarize best-practice compliance results.

recipes.name: recipe-malware-events-review
recipes.description: Review recent malware detection events and statuses.

recipes.name: recipe-malware-scan-start
recipes.description: Start malware scan workflow and track completion.

recipes.name: recipe-license-posture
recipes.description: Summarize installed license and consumption posture.

recipes.name: recipe-config-backup-run
recipes.description: Start configuration backup and validate outcome.

recipes.name: recipe-config-backup-policy-review
recipes.description: Review configuration backup policy and schedule.

recipes.name: recipe-proxy-health-review
recipes.description: List backup proxies and validate operational readiness.

recipes.name: recipe-managed-server-health
recipes.description: List managed servers and identify unavailable hosts.

recipes.name: recipe-protection-group-rescan
recipes.description: Rescan protection group entities and capture upgrade status.

recipes.name: recipe-job-session-export
recipes.description: Export recent job session records for reporting.

recipes.name: recipe-audit-four-eyes-events
recipes.description: Collect authorization events for four-eyes audit evidence.

recipes.name: recipe-restore-point-coverage
recipes.description: Summarize restore-point availability by workload.

recipes.name: recipe-restore-readiness-check
recipes.description: Validate restore paths, mount servers, and key dependencies.

recipes.name: recipe-dr-failover-readiness
recipes.description: Review replica and failover objects for DR readiness.

recipes.name: recipe-dr-failback-status
recipes.description: Inspect failback sessions and unresolved states.

recipes.name: recipe-session-sla-breach
recipes.description: Identify sessions breaching defined completion thresholds.

recipes.name: recipe-encryption-posture
recipes.description: Review encryption and KMS configuration coverage.

recipes.name: recipe-traffic-rules-review
recipes.description: Capture network traffic rule configuration for operations review.

recipes.name: recipe-auditor-monthly-pack
recipes.description: Build a monthly compliance evidence pack for auditors.
